audibility_note: Recipes are conservative morphs from a known-loud template (see recommended_template). Use syx_overlay patch_only so resonance/cutoff can move down as well as up. If silent: verify --syx-template path, use patch_only, and avoid zeroing osc/sustain/cutoff in recipe JSON.

electric_piano.notes: Small deviation set from 28 solid bass; keeps unmapped SysEx bytes (waves, osc freq, etc.) from the template. Do not replace with random full patches or zero-valued params without hardware check.

bass_basic.notes: Darker cutoff, moderate resonance, longer amp decay/sustain for a simple bass line. Morph from 28 solid bass template only.

electric_guitar.notes: Plucky amp, brighter cutoff, filter env for pick attack; conservative morph from 28 solid bass. Real guitar timbre needs template waves/pitch on hardware.

juno_type.notes: Pad-leaning Juno-ish: warm open filter, moderate Q, detuned oscs, gentle LFO. Chorus/sub/wave shapes still come from the template; not a 1:1 Juno clone.

acid_bass.notes: High resonance + strong filter env, snappy attacks, shorter filter decay for squelch. Use a saw (or square) mono bass template; open cutoff on the synth if it is too dark.

acid_bass_v2.notes: V2: brighter base cutoff (78 vs v1), tighter filter decay/sustain for faster squelch, slightly lower amp sustain for pumpier lines, more osc1 bias. Still overlay-only: before export, save a template with Saw A (+ Saw B or square if you like), mono/unison off, glide as needed for slides; this JSON cannot set wave or unison bytes yet.
